title: 5 things hard work builds in a man of God
caption:
Hard work builds more than a career.

The culture measures work by what you produce — the promotion, the paycheck, the flex. But Scripture is more interested in what happens to the *man* while he shows up.

There's character you can't fake and won't have unless you earn it the slow way. Self-mastery that only gets built by showing up on Tuesdays when nobody cares and you do it anyway. Patience that kills entitlement. And capacity for the weight God's planning to put in your hands.

Joseph didn't wait for his purpose to show up. He was faithful on the small stage first — Potiphar's house, the prison floor — and when the kingdom came, he was already the man it required.

"Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men." — Colossians 3:23

The grinding season you're in right now isn't the waiting room. It's the training floor. The question isn't whether the work is worth it. It's whether you're letting it form you.

Do the work.

—

Comment "build" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.
